field_value.value: The software development lifecycle (SDLC) is a structured and iterative methodology used by development teams to build, deliver and maintain high-quality and cost-effective software systems.

To provide a framework for managing and aligning factors such as stakeholder needs, resource availability, and the larger IT environment, facilitating a more streamlined development process, helping stakeholders estimate project costs and time frames, identifying potential challenges, addressing risk factors early, measuring development progress, enhancing documentation and transparency, and better aligning software projects with organizational goals.

value: Each phase has its own objectives and deliverables
Forms a roadmap for software development
Helps stakeholders estimate project costs and time frames
Addresses risk factors early in the development lifecycle
Better aligns software projects with organizational goals
Facilitates a more streamlined development process

field_value.value: The software development lifecycle (SDLC) is the cost-effective and time-efficient process that development teams use to design and build high-quality software. The goal of SDLC is to minimize project risks through forward planning so that software meets customer expectations during production and beyond.
To provide a systematic management framework with specific deliverables at every stage, minimizing project risks through forward planning so that software meets customer expectations.

value: Plan phase – cost‑benefit analysis, scheduling, resource estimation, and allocation
Design phase – analysis of requirements, solution selection, technology choices, and integration planning
Implementation phase – coding the product in manageable tasks
Test phase – automation and manual testing for bugs and quality assurance
Deploy phase – moving builds to production with packaging, configuration, and installation
Maintain phase – bug fixes, issue resolution, performance monitoring, and continuous improvement
Increased visibility of the development process for all stakeholders
Improved risk management and cost estimation
Systematic software delivery and better customer satisfaction

field_value.value: The software development lifecycle (SDLC) is a structured and iterative methodology used by development teams to build, deliver and maintain high-quality and cost-effective software systems.

The SDLC provides a framework for managing and aligning factors such as stakeholder needs, resource availability, and the larger IT environment, helping stakeholders estimate project costs and time frames, identify potential challenges, address risk factors early, measure development progress, enhance documentation and transparency, and better align software projects with organizational goals.

value: The SDLC breaks down software development into distinct, repeatable, interdependent phases.
Each phase of the SDLC has its own objectives and deliverables that guide the next phase.
The phases together form a roadmap that helps development teams create software that meets stakeholder needs, project requirements and customer expectations.
Different SDLC models approach the phases in their own way; for example, the waterfall model completes phases sequentially, while agile models allow phases to be worked on in parallel.
Helps stakeholders estimate project costs and time frames.
Identifies potential challenges and addresses risk factors early in the development lifecycle.
Better aligns software projects with organizational goals.

field_value.value: The software development lifecycle (SDLC) is the cost-effective and time-efficient process that development teams use to design and build high-quality software.
The goal of SDLC is to minimize project risks through forward planning so that software meets customer expectations during production and beyond.

value: Increased visibility of the development process for all stakeholders involved
Improved risk management and cost estimation
Systematic software delivery and better customer satisfaction
Little room for change once a phase is considered complete, as changes can affect delivery time, cost, and quality

field_value.value: The Waterfall model is one of the oldest and most straightforward approaches to software development, following a linear and sequential approach.

value: Phases: Requirements, Design, Development, Testing, Deployment, Maintenance
Waterfall model is based on a series of stages completed in sequence from start to finish
Waterfall analysis stage includes a feasibility study using TELOS (Technical, Economic, Legal, Operational, Scheduling)
Waterfall model is inflexible; changes require revisiting all previous stages
Waterfall users have little input, involved only at analysis and evaluation stages
Agile methodologies are a collection of methodologies aiming to improve flexibility and adapt to changes in user requirements faster
In Agile, the problem is broken down into sections developed in parallel
In Agile, design and analysis phases often occur together
Agile delivers a working prototype early and iteratively improves it
Agile places less focus on documentation and gives more priority to user satisfaction
Agile improves flexibility of software development
Agile adapts to changes in user requirements faster
Agile makes it easier to make improvements or changes to the software
Agile gives higher priority to user satisfaction
Waterfall model is inflexible and unsuitable for projects with changing requirements
Waterfall model provides little user input, only at the beginning and end

field_value.value: DevOps is a software development methodology that accelerates the delivery of high-performance applications and services by combining and automating the work of software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops) teams.
DevOps is about meeting software users’ demands for frequent, innovative new features and uninterrupted performance and availability.

value: Organizational culture shift fostering coordination and collaboration
Iterative, automated workflows within a larger development lifecycle
Shared tools and practices between development and operations teams
Accelerates delivery of high-performance applications and services
Meets user demand for frequent, innovative features
Optimizes rapid delivery of high-quality software
Allows earlier identification and remediation of problems through shift-left testing

field_value.value: The DevOps Lifecycle is a continuous, looping process that bridges the gap between software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops).
DevOps integrates development and operations into a single, infinite loop of collaboration, automation, and feedback.

DevOps is the combination of cultural philosophies, practices, and tools that increases an organization’s ability to deliver applications and services at high velocity: evolving and improving products at a faster pace than organizations using traditional software development and infrastructure management processes.
DevOps is a modern approach to software development that brings development and operations teams together to deliver applications faster and more reliably.
It focuses on collaboration, automation, and continuous improvement across the software lifecycle.

value: delivers applications faster and more reliably
Encourages close collaboration between development and operations teams
Automates build, test, and deployment processes to reduce errors
Enables faster and more frequent software releases
Improves system reliability, monitoring, and feedback loops
Issues are identified early, reducing cost and impact of failures
Continuous monitoring ensures availability and performance

field_value.value: DevOps is a software engineering culture and practice that aims to unify software development and operations. It is an approach to software development that emphasizes collaboration, communication, and integration between software developers and IT operations.

The goal of DevOps was to bring together software development and operations to work collaboratively, improving communication, collaboration, and integration. The focus was on automating manual processes, reducing lead times, and increasing the speed of delivery.

value: reducing lead time for software delivery

field_value.value: A compound of development (Dev) and operations (Ops), DevOps is the union of people, process, and technology to continually provide value to customers.
DevOps enables formerly siloed roles—development, IT operations, quality engineering, and security—to coordinate and collaborate to produce better, more reliable products.

value: Teams that adopt DevOps culture, practices, and tools become high-performing, building better products faster for greater customer satisfaction.
Adapting to the market and competition
Improving the mean time to recovery

field_value.value: DevOps is a software development methodology that accelerates the delivery of high-performance applications and services by combining and automating the work of software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops) teams.
DevOps is about meeting software users’ demands for frequent, innovative new features and uninterrupted performance and availability.

value: collaboration and coordination between development and IT operations teams
DevOps lifecycle consisting of iterative, automated workflows
accelerates delivery of high-performance applications and services
optimizes rapid delivery of high-quality software

field_value.value: The software development lifecycle (SDLC) is a structured and iterative methodology used by development teams to build, deliver and maintain high-quality and cost-effective software systems.
The SDLC provides a framework for managing and aligning stakeholder needs, resource availability, and the larger IT environment, facilitating a more streamlined development process.

value: helps stakeholders estimate project costs and time frames
identifies potential challenges and addresses risk factors early in the development lifecycle
better aligns software projects with organizational goals
produces high-quality and cost-effective software systems

field_value.value: The software development lifecycle (SDLC) is the cost-effective and time-efficient process that development teams use to design and build high-quality software.
The goal of SDLC is to minimize project risks through forward planning so that software meets customer expectations during production and beyond.

value: series of steps that divide the software development process into assignable, completable, and measurable tasks
systematic management framework with specific deliverables at every stage
Increased visibility of the development process for all stakeholders involved
Improved risk management and cost estimation
Systematic software delivery and better customer satisfaction
Little room for change once a phase is considered complete, which can affect delivery time, cost, and quality (as noted for the Waterfall model)

field_value.value: SDLC models are frameworks that guide the development process of software applications from initiation to deployment.

value: Linear and sequential approach to software development
Each phase must be completed before moving on to the next
Highly structured and easy to understand
Phases: Requirements, Design, Development, Testing, Deployment, Maintenance

field_value.value: The DevOps Lifecycle is a continuous, looping process that bridges the gap between software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops).

DevOps is the combination of cultural philosophies, practices, and tools that increases an organization’s ability to deliver applications and services at high velocity: evolving and improving products at a faster pace than organizations using traditional software development and infrastructure management processes.
DevOps is a modern approach to software development that brings development and operations teams together to deliver applications faster and more reliably.
It focuses on collaboration, automation, and continuous improvement across the software lifecycle.

value: Encourages close collaboration between development and operations teams.
Automates build, test, and deployment processes to reduce errors.
Enables faster and more frequent software releases.
Improves system reliability, monitoring, and feedback loops.
Faster and more frequent software releases

field_value.value: DevOps is a software engineering culture and practice that aims to unify software development and operations.
The goal of DevOps was to bring together software development and operations to work collaboratively, improving communication, collaboration, and integration.

value: scaling development faster and more efficiently

field_value.value: A compound of development (Dev) and operations (Ops), DevOps is the union of people, process, and technology to continually provide value to customers.
Unifies people, process, and technology to bring better products to customers faster.

value: union of people, process, and technology
coordination and collaboration across formerly siloed roles such as development, IT operations, quality engineering, and security
high-performing teams building better products faster for greater customer satisfaction
adapting to the market and competition
improving the mean time to recovery

field_value.value: Software Development Life Cycle is a process followed for a software project, within a software organization. It consists of a detailed plan describing how to develop, maintain, replace and alter or enhance specific software.
SDLC is the process consisting of a series of planned activities to develop or alter the software products.
To develop, maintain, replace and enhance specific software.

value: Each phase must be completed before the next phase can begin
Testing is carried out once the code has been fully developed
Requirement is clear before development starts
Each phase is completed in a specified period of time
Proper documentation is followed for quality
Agile methodology provides rapid development and flexibility to adopt new changes during development
Problems with one phase are never solved completely during that phase
Many problems arise after a phase is signed off, leading to a badly structured system
If the client wants the requirement to be changed, it will not be implemented in the current development process

field_value.value: The systems development life cycle (SDLC), also referred to as the application development life-cycle, is a term used in systems engineering, information systems and software engineering to describe a process for planning, creating, testing, and deploying an information system.
This paper provides a comparison of various SDLC models of software, including their advantages, disadvantages, limitations, and best use according to situation.

value: Feasibility Study phase examines the feasibility of the system.
Requirement understanding phase involves gathering and analysis of requirements.
System Design phase creates detailed analysis and design based on user requirements.
Development phase involves coding the design into a programming language.
System Testing phase tests the implemented system to remove bugs.
Maintenance phase eliminates errors and adapts the system to future enhancements.
